recipes.title: Accordion Sweet Potatoes
recipes.body:
## Notes

Use wooden chopsticks as guides when slicing the sweet potatoes to prevent cutting completely through them.

Cutting vertically on one side and diagonally on the other creates the accordion shape.

Save the sweet potato offcuts and roast them separately to avoid waste.

Serve immediately while hot for the crispiest texture.

## Instructions

1. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F) fan-forced and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
2. Peel the sweet potatoes and trim the tops, bottoms, and sides to create flat edges.
3. Slice each sweet potato lengthwise into 3-4 evenly sized thick pieces.
4. Place one sweet potato slice between two wooden chopsticks.
5. Make thin vertical cuts across the sweet potato, using the chopsticks to prevent the knife from cutting completely through.
6. Flip the sweet potato slice over and make thin diagonal cuts across the opposite side.
7. Repeat with the remaining sweet potato slices and arrange them on the prepared baking tray.
8. In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, sea salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, and rosemary.
9. Brush the seasoned olive oil generously over the sweet potatoes, making sure the oil gets between the cuts.
10. Bake for 30 minutes, or until the side facing upward is golden brown.
11. Flip the sweet potatoes and bake for another 20 minutes, or until golden and crispy.
12. Transfer to a serving plate and garnish with fresh parsley.
13. Serve immediately while hot and crispy.

recipes.title: Crispy Air Fryer Sushi Rice Paper Wraps
ingredients: soy sauce, for marinating the tofu
recipes.body:
## Notes

Marinate the tofu in soy sauce for 30 minutes before assembling the wraps.

Do not soak the rice paper for too long. Dip it briefly in water so it remains slightly firm and becomes easier to wrap as it softens.

Avoid overfilling the wraps, as this can make them difficult to seal and more likely to burst while air frying.

## Instructions

1. Cut the tofu into strips and marinate in soy sauce for 30 minutes.
2. Prepare the sushi rice according to package instructions and allow it to cool slightly.
3. Thinly slice the cucumber and carrots.
4. Dip one rice paper wrapper briefly in water and place it on a clean, flat surface.
5. Place a piece of nori in the center of the rice paper.
6. Add a layer of sushi rice, cucumber, carrots, and marinated tofu.
7. Place another piece of nori on top of the filling.
8. Fold the sides of the rice paper inward and tightly wrap around the filling.
9. Brush or lightly coat the outside of the wrap with sesame oil.
10. Sprinkle sesame seeds over the outside and gently press them onto the rice paper.
11. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
12. Place the wraps in the air fryer basket in a single layer, leaving space between each wrap.
13. Air fry at 350°F (175°C) for 8-10 minutes, or until golden and crispy.
14. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Anchovy Straws
recipes.body:
## Notes

Keep the puff pastry cold while working with it. Freezing the assembled pastry for 15 minutes makes it easier to cut clean, even straws.

## Instructions

1. Brush one side of both sheets of puff pastry with beaten egg.
2. Arrange the anchovies from the entire tin in three evenly spaced ribbons across one sheet of puff pastry.
3. Sprinkle with Aleppo pepper and finely grated Parmesan.
4. Place the second sheet of puff pastry on top, egg-washed side facing down, to create a sandwich.
5. Transfer to the freezer for 15 minutes to firm up.
6. Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
7. Remove the pastry from the freezer and cut into 1 cm wide straws.
8. Place the straws on their sides on the lined baking tray.
9. Bake for 15 minutes, or until puffed, crisp, and golden brown.
10. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Arayes – Meat Stuffed Pita
recipes.body:
## Notes

NA

## Instructions

1. Mix the feta cheese, herbs, and spices with the ground beef.
2. Cut the pita in half. Fill with meat and spread out evenly to desired thinness or thickness
3. Brush all sides of the filled pita with oil.
4. Preheat a pan or skillet over medium high heat.
5. Cook the arayes on each side (top, booth, and the open end) for 2-3 minutes until a dark golden brown color forms.
6. Slice in half and serve!

recipes.title: Butterfly Pea Mini Focaccia
ingredients: 1 tablespoon (25 g) levain or 2 g instant yeast
1/2 cup (100 g) butterfly pea flower tea
sweet potato, thinly sliced, for topping
butter, cut into small cubes, for topping
recipes.body:
## Notes

This recipe makes one 6-inch personal focaccia.

Use either 25 g levain or 2 g instant yeast.

An overnight cold fermentation after the coil folds is optional and can help develop more flavor.

The exact proofing time will depend on the temperature of your kitchen and whether you use levain or instant yeast. During the second proof, allow the dough to rise until almost doubled in size.

## Instructions

1. In a bowl, combine the bread flour, butterfly pea flower tea, and levain or instant yeast.
2. Mix until no dry flour remains, then cover and let the dough rest for 30 minutes.
3. Add the salt and olive oil and work them into the dough until incorporated.
4. Cover and let the dough rest for another 30 minutes.
5. Perform one set of coil folds by gently lifting and folding the dough underneath itself.
6. Cover and rest for 30 minutes.
7. Repeat the coil folds two more times, resting the dough for 30 minutes between each set.
8. Optionally, cover the dough and refrigerate overnight for a cold fermentation.
9. Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled 6-inch baking dish.
10. Gently stretch the dough toward the edges of the dish without tearing it.
11. Cover and proof until the dough has almost doubled in size.
12. Preheat the oven to 230°C (450°F).
13. Drizzle the dough with olive oil and press your fingers deeply into the surface to create dimples.
14. Top with thinly sliced sweet potato, sage leaves, butter cubes, and smoked sea salt.
15. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the focaccia is cooked through and lightly browned.
16. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Butternut Squash and Feta Soup
ingredients: 1 butternut squash, peeled and cut into cubes
additional feta cheese, for the feta crisp
1 tablespoon olive oil, plus more as needed
recipes.body:
## Notes

Roasting the butternut squash and feta before blending gives the soup a deeper, richer flavor.

Adjust the amount of vegetable stock depending on your preferred soup consistency.

The feta crisp can be prepared while the soup is simmering.

## Instructions

1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. Peel and cut the butternut squash into cubes.
3. Place the butternut squash and block of feta on a baking tray.
4. Drizzle with olive oil and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until the butternut squash is tender and lightly browned.
5. Transfer the roasted butternut squash and feta to a large pot.
6. Add the vegetable stock and simmer for a few minutes.
7. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. Add additional vegetable stock if needed to reach your preferred consistency.
8. Reduce the oven temperature to 150°C (300°F).
9. To prepare the feta crisp, place additional feta in a bowl with the za'atar and a drizzle of olive oil.
10. Mash until combined, then spread the mixture into a thin layer on a parchment-lined baking tray.
11. Bake for 15 minutes, or until lightly golden and crisp around the edges.
12. Allow the feta crisp to cool slightly, then break into pieces.
13. Serve the soup in bowls topped with the feta crisp, fresh dill, and chili oil.

recipes.title: Cheesecake Stuffed Dates
recipes.body:
## Notes

Make sure the dates are completely dry before filling and coating them so the melted chocolate sticks properly.

Keep the cheesecake dates refrigerated until ready to serve.

## Instructions

1. Wash and thoroughly dry the dates.
2. Slice each date lengthwise and remove the pit, keeping the date intact.
3. Set aside 1 tablespoon of the crushed Lotus Biscoff biscuits for decorating.
4. In a small bowl, combine the mascarpone, remaining crushed biscuits, and vanilla extract.
5. Transfer the mascarpone mixture to a piping bag or use a small spoon to fill the center of each date.
6. Gently press the dates closed around the filling.
7. Melt the white chocolate until smooth.
8. Dip each filled date into the melted white chocolate until completely coated.
9. Place the chocolate-coated dates on a parchment-lined tray.
10. Sprinkle with the reserved crushed Lotus Biscoff biscuits before the chocolate sets.
11. Refrigerate until the chocolate is firm and keep chilled until ready to serve.

recipes.title: Chicken and Leek Soup with Parmesan Dumplings
ingredients: 1 pound leeks, white and light green parts only, halved lengthwise, sliced 1/2 inch thick, and thoroughly washed
recipes.body:
## Notes

Use only the white and light green parts of the leeks and wash them thoroughly after slicing to remove any dirt trapped between the layers.

Do not stir the dumplings during the first 2 minutes of cooking so they have time to set.

Pecorino Romano can be substituted for Parmesan.

For easier preparation, trim, slice, and wash the leeks, grate the Parmesan, and shred the rotisserie chicken in advance.

Garnish with chopped fresh parsley if desired.

## Instructions

1. Melt the butter in a Dutch oven or large pot over medium-high heat.
2. Add the sliced leeks and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
3. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the leeks are softened and beginning to brown.
4. Add the minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute, or until fragrant.
5. Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
6. Reduce the heat to medium.
7. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, grated Parmesan, water, beaten egg, remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, baking powder, and black pepper.
8. Mix until a thick dumpling dough forms.
9. Using two spoons, scrape rough tablespoon-sized portions of dumpling dough directly into the simmering soup.
10. Cook the dumplings without stirring for 2 minutes.
11. Gently stir to separate any dumplings that have stuck together.
12. Continue cooking for another 2 minutes.
13. Carefully stir in the shredded rotisserie chicken.
14. Cook for about 1 minute, or until the chicken is heated through.
15. Divide among bowls, garnish with chopped parsley if desired, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Cinnamon French Toast Rolls
recipes.body:
## Notes

Soft sandwich bread works best because it is easier to flatten and roll without tearing.

The cinnamon butter should be spread in a thin layer to prevent it from leaking excessively while cooking.

Nutella, powdered sugar, and strawberries are optional toppings.

## Instructions

1. In a small bowl, combine the melted butter, brown sugar, and 1/2 tablespoon cinnamon to make the cinnamon butter.
2. In a separate shallow bowl, whisk together the egg, milk, and vanilla extract.
3. In another small bowl, combine the granulated sugar and remaining 1/2 tablespoon cinnamon to make the cinnamon sugar.
4. Cut the crusts from the slices of bread.
5. Flatten each slice of bread with a rolling pin or the side of a drinking glass.
6. Spread a thin layer of cinnamon butter over each slice of bread.
7. Roll each slice tightly into a cylinder.
8. Dip each roll into the egg and milk mixture, coating all sides.
9. Melt a small amount of butter in a frying pan over medium heat.
10. Add the rolls and cook, turning regularly, until all sides are golden brown.
11. Remove the rolls from the pan and coat with the cinnamon sugar while still warm.
12. Drizzle with Nutella and sprinkle with powdered sugar if desired.
13. Garnish with sliced strawberries and serve warm.

recipes.title: Clementine Vodka Smash
recipes.body:
## Notes

Vodka creates a clean, citrus-forward cocktail, gin adds botanical notes, and bourbon creates a richer and warmer drink.

To make honey syrup, combine equal parts honey and warm water and stir until completely dissolved.

For a mocktail version, omit the alcohol and add additional soda water or a non-alcoholic spirit.

Double-straining removes the muddled clementine pulp, seeds, and pieces of thyme from the finished cocktail.

## Instructions

1. Add the clementine, thyme sprigs, lemon juice, honey syrup, and vodka, gin, or bourbon to a cocktail shaker.
2. Muddle thoroughly to release the clementine juice and the aroma from the thyme.
3. Fill the cocktail shaker with ice.
4. Shake vigorously until well chilled.
5. Fill a serving glass with fresh ice.
6. Double-strain the cocktail into the prepared glass.
7. Top with soda water.
8. Garnish with a fresh clementine slice and a sprig of thyme.
9. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Crispy Pan-Fried Onigirazu
ingredients: 3 cups cooked short-grain white rice, seasoned with salt
recipes.body:
## Notes

Fair warning: regular tuna sandwiches are never going to hit the same after this.

## Instructions

1. Mix tuna with mayo, Sriracha, salt, and pepper. Set aside.
2. Fry the eggs and prep all fillings before building.
3. Dip 2 rice paper sheets in warm water, then overlap them on a cutting board. Layer 2 nori sheets on top.
4. Cut 2 slits to create 4 sections. Place rice in one section, tuna across from it, egg in the third, and avocado and cucumber in the fourth.
5. Fold rice over avocado, fold that stack onto the tuna, fold tuna over the egg, fold egg over to the rice, then fold rice over cucumber.
6. Wrap the whole square in 2 more wet rice paper sheets, press to seal, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
7. Pan fry in neutral oil on medium-high heat, searing all sides until golden and crispy.
8. Slice in half.

recipes.title: Crunchy Mediterranean Roast Potatoes
ingredients: 4 potatoes, peeled and cut into cubes
1 teaspoon honey, plus more for serving
recipes.body:
## Notes

Parboiling the potatoes and shaking them after draining roughens the edges, helping them become extra crispy in the oven.

Preheating the vegetable oil in the baking tray helps the potatoes begin crisping as soon as they are added.

The lemon, oregano, and honey sauce can also be used as a dressing for salads, pasta, or other roasted vegetables.

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into evenly sized cubes.
2. Place the potatoes in a pot, cover with water, add a sprinkle of salt, and bring to a boil. Cook for 15 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
4. During the last 5 minutes of boiling the potatoes, pour enough vegetable oil into a deep baking tray to just cover the bottom. Place the tray in the oven for 5 minutes to heat the oil.
5. Meanwhile, combine the lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, chili flakes, salt, and honey in a small bowl. Mix well and set aside.
6. Drain the potatoes thoroughly and shake them vigorously in the pot or colander to roughen and fluff the edges.
7. Carefully add the potatoes to the baking tray with the hot oil and turn them to coat evenly.
8. Roast for 20 minutes, then remove the tray from the oven and turn the potatoes.
9. Return to the oven for another 20 minutes. Remove and turn the potatoes again.
10. Roast for another 20 minutes, then remove the tray and turn the potatoes once more.
11. Drizzle half of the lemon, oregano, and honey sauce over the potatoes and toss well to coat.
12. Return the potatoes to the oven for 5 minutes.
13. Allow the potatoes to cool slightly, then sprinkle generously with crumbled feta.
14. Drizzle with additional honey and serve with the remaining sauce as desired.

recipes.title: Curried Carrot Noodle Soup
ingredients: 5 large carrots, cut into 2 cm chunks
recipes.body:
## Notes

Blend the carrots, coconut milk, and vegetable broth until completely smooth for a creamy soup base.

The soup will thicken as the vermicelli noodles absorb the liquid. Add additional vegetable broth or water if you prefer a thinner consistency.

## Instructions

1. Cut the carrots into approximately 2 cm chunks.
2. Place the carrots in a pot and add enough water to cover them.
3. Bring to a boil, reduce the heat to medium, and cook for about 15 minutes, or until the carrots are tender.
4. Drain the carrots and transfer them to a blender.
5. Add the coconut milk, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper.
6. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. Set aside.
7. Place the vermicelli rice noodles in a heatproof bowl and cover with hot water.
8. Let the noodles sit for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
9. Heat a small amount of olive oil in a large pot or deep skillet over medium-high heat.
10. Add the diced onion and sauté for 2-3 minutes, or until softened.
11. Add the garlic, Thai red curry paste, and drained chickpeas.
12. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently.
13. Pour in the blended carrot soup mixture and add the cooked vermicelli noodles.
14. Reduce the heat to low-medium and cook for 5-10 minutes, stirring regularly, until the soup has thickened slightly and is heated through.
15. Remove from the heat and serve hot.
16. Garnish with coconut cream, fresh herbs, and crushed nuts if desired.

recipes.title: Curried Chicken Sandwiches with Apple-Yogurt Slaw
ingredients: 1 Granny Smith apple, peeled and shredded
recipes.body:
## Notes

Salting and draining the shredded apple removes excess moisture and prevents the yogurt slaw from becoming watery.

Allow the cooked chicken to rest for 5 minutes before slicing to help retain its juices.

Any variety of apple can be substituted for Granny Smith.

Habanero can be substituted for jalapeño for additional heat.

Fresh mint can be substituted for cilantro.

Pita bread or crusty bread can be substituted for naan.

The cooked chicken can be refrigerated for up to 3 days.

For easier preparation, peel and shred the apple, mince the jalapeño, and wash and chop the cilantro in advance.

## Instructions

1. Adjust an oven rack to the middle position and preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Place the shredded apple in a colander and toss with 1/4 teaspoon salt.
3. Let the apple drain for 10 minutes, then press gently to remove excess liquid.
4. Meanwhile, arrange the naan breads on a rimmed baking sheet.
5. Bake for about 10 minutes, or until lightly golden and beginning to crisp.
6. In a shallow dish, combine the flour, curry powder, cayenne pepper, and remaining 1 teaspoon salt.
7. Pat the chicken cutlets dry with paper towels.
8. Dredge each cutlet in the seasoned flour mixture and shake off the excess.
9. Heat the oil in a 12-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat until just smoking.
10. Add the chicken cutlets and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
11. Transfer the chicken to a cutting board, loosely tent with aluminum foil, and let rest for 5 minutes.
12. Slice the chicken on the bias into 1/2-inch-thick pieces.
13. In a medium bowl, combine the drained apple, Greek yogurt, jalapeño, cilantro, and grated ginger.
14. Season the apple-yogurt slaw with salt and black pepper to taste.
15. Divide the sliced chicken evenly among the naan breads, arranging the chicken over one half of each naan.
16. Top the chicken with the apple-yogurt slaw.
17. Fold the naan over the filling and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Baked Eggs with Mushrooms and Goat Cheese Cream
recipes.body:
## Notes

Bake the eggs in a water bath to ensure gentle, even cooking.

The eggs are ready when the whites are set but the yolks are still soft and runny. Adjust the cooking time depending on your preferred yolk consistency.

Serve immediately with toasted bread for dipping.

## Instructions

1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. In a small bowl, mash the fresh goat cheese with the heavy cream until smooth and creamy. Refrigerate until needed.
3. Heat a skillet over medium heat and sauté the mushrooms with the garlic and chopped parsley until the mushrooms are tender and lightly browned.
4. Divide the goat cheese cream among oven-safe ramekins.
5. Add the sautéed mushrooms on top of the goat cheese cream.
6. Crack one egg into each ramekin.
7. Place the ramekins in a deep baking dish.
8. Carefully pour hot water into the baking dish until it reaches approximately halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
9. Transfer the baking dish to the oven and bake for about 10 minutes, or until the egg whites are set and the yolks are cooked to your liking.
10. Carefully remove the ramekins from the water bath and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Fondant Potatoes
ingredients: 4 russet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch rounds
recipes.body:
## Notes

Soaking the potatoes in cold water helps remove excess starch. Dry the potatoes thoroughly before searing to help them develop a deep golden-brown crust.

Use an oven-safe skillet so the potatoes can be transferred directly from the stovetop to the oven.

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and trim the ends. Cut each potato in half crosswise to create 8 flat potato rounds.
2. Place the potatoes in a large bowl and cover with cold water. Soak for 20 minutes.
3. Arrange an oven rack in the middle of the oven and preheat to 400°F.
4. Drain the potatoes, rinse with cold water, and pat thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season with salt and black pepper.
5. Heat avocado oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Add the potatoes, cut side down, and cook without moving for about 7 minutes, or until deeply golden brown.
7. Flip the potatoes and add the smashed garlic, butter, rosemary, and thyme.
8. Cook for 2-3 minutes, spooning the foaming butter over the potatoes, until the butter begins to brown.
9. Add the chicken stock so the potatoes are approximately halfway submerged. Bring the stock to a simmer.
10. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are fork tender and lightly browned on all sides.
11. Spoon the garlic-herb butter and reduced cooking liquid over the potatoes and serve hot.

recipes.title: French Beer Crêpes
ingredients: 1/2 cup wheat beer or blond beer
recipes.body:
## Notes

Allow the batter to rest for 30 minutes before cooking. This helps the flour fully hydrate and produces more tender crêpes.

Wheat beer or blond beer works best. The beer helps create lighter crêpes and adds a richer flavor.

If you do not want to use beer, replace it with an equal amount of additional milk.

The number of crêpes will depend on the size of your pan and how thinly you spread the batter.

## Instructions

1. Melt the butter and allow it to cool slightly.
2. In a large bowl, whisk the eggs, melted butter, maple syrup, and vanilla extract until combined.
3. Add the flour and salt and mix until incorporated.
4. Gradually pour in the whole milk while whisking to create a smooth batter.
5. Add the beer and whisk gently until the batter is smooth and evenly combined.
6. Cover the batter and let it rest for 30 minutes at room temperature.
7. Heat a lightly buttered crêpe pan or non-stick skillet over medium heat.
8. Pour a small amount of batter into the center of the pan and immediately tilt and rotate the pan to spread the batter into a thin, even layer.
9. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the bottom is golden and the edges begin to lift from the pan.
10. Flip the crêpe and cook for another 1-2 minutes, or until lightly golden.
11. Transfer the cooked crêpe to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter, adding more butter to the pan as needed.
12. Serve warm with your preferred sweet or savory toppings.

recipes.title: Green Chicken Chili
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
recipes.body:
## Notes

Mash some of the cannellini beans against the side of the pot to naturally thicken the chili without adding flour or cornstarch.

Four large shallots can be substituted for the onion.

Small white beans can be substituted for the cannellini beans.

Serve with lime wedges for additional acidity if desired.

The chili can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 1 month.

For easier preparation, chop the onion, wash and chop the cilantro, and shred the rotisserie chicken in advance.

## Instructions

1. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven or large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
2. Add the finely chopped onion, black pepper, and salt.
3. Cook for about 4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened.
4. Add the minced garlic and ground cumin.
5. Cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
6. Increase the heat to medium-high.
7. Add the chicken broth, cannellini beans, and green salsa.
8. Stir to combine and bring the chili to a boil.
9. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to develop.
10. Using the back of a wooden spoon, mash some of the beans against the side of the pot until the chili is slightly thickened.
11. Stir in the shredded rotisserie chicken and chopped cilantro.
12. Cook for about 2 minutes, or until the chicken is heated through.
13. Divide the chili among bowls.
14. Top each serving with 1 tablespoon sour cream.
15. Serve hot with lime wedges if desired.

recipes.title: Italian Greyhound Spritz
recipes.body:
## Notes

Use gin for a more botanical flavor or vodka for a cleaner, more neutral cocktail.

Campari will create a more bitter spritz, while Aperol will make the drink sweeter and less bitter.

For decorative ice cubes, freeze fresh edible marigold flowers in an ice mold with water. Make sure the flowers are food-safe and have not been treated with pesticides.

## Instructions

1. Fill a glass with ice.
2. Add the gin or vodka, fresh grapefruit juice, and Campari or Aperol.
3. Top with sparkling water.
4. Stir gently until combined.
5. Garnish with an edible marigold flower and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Cavolo Nero and Chickpea Stew with Stracciatella
ingredients: 1 jar (700 g) chickpeas, with their brine
1 heaped tablespoon stracciatella or other soft cheese (mozzarella or ricotta)
recipes.body:
## Notes

Blanching the cavolo nero and immediately transferring it to ice water helps preserve its vibrant green color.

Blend the cavolo nero with a small amount of olive oil and enough water to create a smooth, pourable mixture.

Use the chickpea brine in the stew, as it adds body and helps create a creamier consistency.

Stracciatella can be substituted with torn fresh mozzarella, burrata, ricotta, or another soft cheese.

Serve with crusty bread for scooping up the stew.

## Instructions

1. Dice the onion and heat a generous drizzle of olive oil in a large pan or pot over medium-low heat.
2. Add the onion and cook gently until soft and translucent.
3. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
4. Add the cavolo nero, including the stalks, and blanch for about 2 minutes.
5. Transfer the cavolo nero immediately to a bowl of ice water to stop the cooking process.
6. Drain the cavolo nero and squeeze out most of the excess water.
7. Transfer the cavolo nero to a blender with a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt.
8. Blend until smooth, adding a small amount of water as needed to loosen the mixture. Set aside.
9. Add the minced garlic to the softened onions and cook for another 2 minutes.
10. Pour the chickpeas and their brine into the pan.
11. Add the blended cavolo nero and stir until combined.
12. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
13. Taste and season with salt and black pepper as needed.
14. Divide the stew among bowls and top with stracciatella.
15. Finish with lemon zest, lemon juice, chopped chili, and a generous drizzle of olive oil.
16. Serve hot with crusty bread.

recipes.title: King Oyster Mushroom Skewers
recipes.body:
## Notes

Slice the king oyster mushroom lengthwise into thin, even pieces so they cook quickly and can be easily threaded onto skewers.

Soaking wooden skewers in water before cooking can help prevent them from burning.

Adjust the amount of chili crisp depending on your preferred spice level.

## Instructions

1. Thinly slice the king oyster mushroom lengthwise.
2. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil.
3. Add the mushroom slices and boil for 2 minutes.
4. Drain thoroughly and allow the mushrooms to cool slightly.
5. Thread the mushroom slices onto skewers.
6. Heat a small amount of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
7. Add the mushroom skewers and pan-fry until golden brown on both sides.
8. In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, chili crisp, and sesame oil.
9. Pour or brush the sauce over the mushroom skewers and cook briefly until the mushrooms are evenly coated and the sauce has caramelized slightly.
10. Transfer to a plate, garnish with sesame seeds, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Korean Vegetable Pancakes
ingredients: 1/2 cup red cabbage, thinly sliced into 1-inch pieces
1 jalapeño, seeds removed and thinly sliced into 1-inch strips
recipes.body:
## Notes

Slice the vegetables thinly so they cook through quickly and hold together well in the pancakes.

Flatten the pancakes with a spatula after adding them to the pan to create thin, crispy pancakes.

Transfer the cooked pancakes to a paper towel-lined plate to absorb excess oil.

Serve hot with the dipping sauce.

## Instructions

1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and kosher salt.
2. Add the water and mix until a smooth batter forms.
3. Add the leek greens, carrot, scallions, red cabbage, and jalapeño.
4. Mix until the vegetables are evenly coated in the batter.
5. In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, lemon juice, water, sesame oil, and sesame seeds. Set aside.
6. Heat the avocado oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
7. Scoop approximately 1/4 cup of batter into the pan for each pancake.
8. Flatten each pancake slightly with a spatula.
9. Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.
10. Flip and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
11. Transfer the cooked pancakes to a paper towel-lined plate to absorb excess oil.
12. Repeat with the remaining batter.
13. Serve hot with the dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Laxpudding
recipes.body:
## Notes

NA

## Instructions

1. Peel the potatoes and boil in salted water until fork-tender. Let cool, then slice.
2. Thinly slice the onion and cook in the butter until soft, reserving the melted butter.
3. Whisk together cream, milk, and eggs, seasoning with salt and pepper.
4. Layer the sliced potatoes, smoked salmon, cooked onions, and fresh dill in a baking dish, pouring a bit of the cream mixture between each layer. Finish with a final layer of the cream mixture on top.
5. Bake at 175°C for about 30 minutes, until the top is golden and bubbly.
6. Drizzle with the reserved melted butter and garnish with a sprinkle of fresh dill.
I love serving it with a simple green salad tossed in lemon juice. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Finnish Salmon Soup (Lohikeitto)
ingredients: 500 g salmon, skin removed and cut into large cubes
400 g firm potatoes, cut into cubes
3 leeks, thinly sliced and thoroughly washed
1 large bunch fresh dill, finely chopped
butter or olive oil, for cooking
recipes.body:
## Notes

Use a thick salmon fillet so it can be cut into large cubes without falling apart during cooking.

Fish stock gives the soup the most traditional flavor, but vegetable stock or chicken stock can be substituted.

Do not allow the cream to boil after adding it to the soup.

The soup can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently over low heat or in the microwave at medium power.

Freezing is not recommended because the cream may separate and the potatoes can change texture.

Serve with rye bread and additional fresh dill.

## Instructions

1. Thinly slice the leeks and wash them thoroughly. Peel and slice the carrots, cut the potatoes into cubes, and cut the salmon into large cubes.
2. Finely chop the fresh dill and set aside.
3. Heat butter or olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
4. Add the leeks and carrots and sauté until the leeks have softened and reduced in volume by about half.
5. Add the potatoes and fish stock. Season with salt and black pepper.
6. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook over medium heat for about 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
7. Add the salmon cubes and heavy cream.
8. Cook gently over medium heat for 3-5 minutes, or until the salmon is just cooked through. Do not allow the cream to boil.
9. Remove the pot from the heat, cover with a lid, and let the soup rest for 5 minutes.
10. Stir in most of the chopped fresh dill.
11. Serve in bowls topped with additional dill, freshly ground black pepper, and a drizzle of lemon juice.

recipes.title: Meal Prep Soup Jars
ingredients: 2 cups cooked protein (chicken, pork, beef, or tofu), shredded or cubed
4 teaspoons Better than Bouillon chicken soup base (or 4 chicken bouillon cubes)
recipes.body:
## Notes

Store the prepared soup jars in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.

Use 16-ounce heat-safe glass storage jars.

Quick-cook rice vermicelli or ramen noodles work best because they soften when hot water is added.

Allow the jars to come to room temperature before adding hot water to reduce the risk of the glass shattering. The water should be hot but not boiling.

## Instructions

1. To each 16-ounce glass jar, add 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon miso paste, 1 teaspoon sriracha, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon Better than Bouillon chicken soup base (or 1 chicken bouillon cube), 1/4 teaspoon grated ginger, and 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder.
2. Mix the soup base ingredients with a small whisk or fork until well combined into a paste.
3. Divide the cooked protein evenly among the four jars.
4. Divide the carrots, bell peppers, edamame, and bok choy among the jars, adding the sturdier vegetables first.
5. Top each jar with green onions and cilantro.
6. Add one serving of quick-cook noodles to the top of each jar.
7. Seal the jars with tight-fitting lids and refrigerate for up to 3-4 days.
8. When ready to serve, remove a jar from the refrigerator and allow it to come to room temperature.
9. Slowly pour hot, but not boiling, water into the jar until all the ingredients are covered.
10. Close the lid and let the soup sit for 10 minutes. Use oven mitts or a towel when handling the hot jar.
11. Open the jar and stir thoroughly to dissolve the soup base and combine all the ingredients.
12. Enjoy directly from the jar or transfer to a bowl.

recipes.title: Mushroom Bourguignon
ingredients: 1 kg mixed mushrooms (button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms, portobello mushrooms, or a combination), sliced
recipes.body:
## Notes

Use a mixture of mushrooms for a greater variety of textures and flavors.

Allow the mushrooms to cook down and release their moisture before adding the flour and liquids.

Serve with mashed potatoes, polenta, pasta, or crusty bread.

## Instructions

1. Finely chop the onion and shallot. Slice the carrots and mushrooms.
2. Melt the butter in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
3. Add the onion, shallot, and carrots and cook for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add the mushrooms and continue cooking until they have released their moisture and reduced in volume.
5. Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir well to coat everything evenly.
6. Add the vegetable stock and red wine, stirring well to prevent lumps from forming.
7. Add the rosemary, thyme, and bay leaf.
8. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
9. Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
10. Remove the rosemary stems, thyme stems, and bay leaf.
11. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
12. Serve hot with mashed potatoes or your preferred side.

recipes.title: Lion's Mane Mushroom Steak Sandwich
recipes.body:
## Notes

The mushrooms can be marinated for anywhere from 30 minutes to 2 hours. A longer marinating time allows the mushrooms to absorb more flavor.

Pressing the lion's mane mushrooms while cooking removes moisture, creates a denser texture, and helps develop a golden-brown crust.

Use vegan ciabatta and crispy onions if you want to keep the entire sandwich vegan.

## Instructions

1. In a large tray, combine the beetroot juice, dark soy sauce, smoked paprika, maple syrup or agave, olive oil, brown miso paste, salt, and garlic powder. Mix well to make the marinade.
2. Heat a small amount of oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
3. Add the lion's mane mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes on each side.
4. As the mushrooms soften, gradually press them down with a spatula or another heavy pan to release moisture and flatten them into steak-like pieces.
5. Continue cooking and pressing until the mushrooms are golden brown on both sides.
6. Transfer the mushrooms to the marinade and turn to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 30 minutes to 2 hours.
7. Meanwhile, combine the wholegrain mustard, vegan mayonnaise, and chopped parsley in a small bowl.
8. Slice and toast the ciabatta bread if desired.
9. Heat a small amount of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
10. Remove the mushrooms from the marinade and fry until deeply browned and charred around the edges.
11. Spread the mustard mayonnaise onto the ciabatta.
12. Add the rocket, charred lion's mane mushrooms, and crispy onions.
13. Close the sandwich and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Beef Pho Broth
ingredients: 5 pounds beef marrow bones and knuckle bones
1/4 cup fish sauce, plus more to taste
recipes.body:
## Notes

Keep the broth at a gentle simmer rather than a hard boil to produce a clear broth. Skim the surface regularly, especially during the first 1-2 hours of cooking.

The broth should taste slightly stronger and saltier than you want the finished bowl to taste because the rice noodles, meat, and garnishes will dilute the flavor.

## Instructions

1. Place the beef bones in a large stockpot and cover with cold water. Bring to a vigorous boil and boil for 3-5 minutes to release impurities.
2. Drain the bones and discard the water. Rinse the bones thoroughly with warm water and wash the stockpot to remove any residue.
3. Return the cleaned bones to the stockpot and add 5-6 quarts of fresh water. Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to maintain a gentle simmer.
4. Skim any foam, fat, and impurities that rise to the surface during the first hour of cooking.
5. Cut the onions and ginger in half. Char them under the broiler, over a gas flame, or in a dry cast-iron skillet until blackened in spots and fragrant.
6. Toast the star anise, cloves, cinnamon stick, and coriander seeds in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Place the spices in a spice bag or piece of cheesecloth.
7. Add the charred onions, ginger, beef chuck or brisket, and spice bag to the stockpot.
8. Simmer gently for 1 1/2 hours, skimming the surface as needed.
9. Remove the beef chuck or brisket when tender. Let cool, thinly slice, and refrigerate for serving with the finished pho.
10. Continue simmering the bones, onions, ginger, and spices for another 2-4 hours. Do not allow the broth to boil vigorously.
11. Remove the bones, onions, ginger, and spice bag. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean pot.
12. Add the rock sugar, salt, and fish sauce. Stir until the sugar dissolves, then taste and adjust the seasoning with additional fish sauce, salt, or sugar as needed.
13. If the broth has reduced significantly, add enough water to bring it back to approximately 5 quarts.
14. Serve hot over cooked rice noodles with thinly sliced beef and your preferred pho garnishes.

recipes.title: Phyllo Feta Cigars
ingredients: 10-12 sheets phyllo dough, thawed if frozen
recipes.body:
## Notes

Keep unused phyllo dough covered with a lightly damp kitchen towel while assembling the cigars to prevent it from drying out.

Place the filling along the short edge of each phyllo rectangle for crispier cigars.

For a vegan version, substitute plant-based cheese for the feta, use an appropriate egg substitute or omit the eggs if the cheese mixture holds together, and use olive oil or melted plant-based butter instead of butter.

Serve warm with a drizzle of honey if desired.

## Instructions

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large bowl, combine the crumbled feta, eggs, dill, chives, salt, and black pepper.
3. Mix until the filling is evenly combined.
4. Place one sheet of phyllo dough on a clean work surface and brush lightly with melted butter.
5. Place a second sheet of phyllo directly on top and brush lightly with melted butter.
6. Cut the layered phyllo sheets into four evenly sized rectangles.
7. Place approximately 1 tablespoon of feta filling along the short edge of each rectangle, leaving about 1/2 inch of space on each side.
8. Roll the phyllo over the filling, then fold the sides inward halfway through rolling.
9. Continue rolling tightly to form a cigar shape.
10. Repeat with the remaining phyllo dough and filling.
11. Place the cigars seam side down on a baking sheet.
12. Brush the tops lightly with additional melted butter.
13. Bake for 30 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and crispy.
14. Allow to cool slightly and serve warm with honey if desired.

recipes.title: Red Lentil Protein Wraps
recipes.body:
## Notes

Each wrap contains approximately 9 g of protein and 112 calories, depending on the ingredients and brands used.

The lentils should soak for at least 1 hour before blending.

The batter will thicken as it sits. Blend it for a few seconds between cooking wraps if needed to restore a smooth consistency.

A high-quality non-stick pan may not require any oil.

## Instructions

1. Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under running water.
2. Place the lentils in a bowl with the warm water and soak for at least 1 hour.
3. Transfer the lentils and soaking water to a blender.
4. Add the spinach, nutritional yeast, and salt.
5. Blend for about 2 minutes, or until the batter is completely smooth and lump-free.
6. Heat a small amount of oil in a non-stick pan or skillet. If using a high-quality non-stick pan, the oil can be omitted.
7. Reduce the heat to medium-low and pour enough batter into the pan to create a thin layer.
8. Use the back of a spoon to spread the batter evenly across the surface of the pan if necessary.
9. Cook for 3-4 minutes, or until the edges begin to lift away from the pan.
10. Flip the wrap and cook for another 1-2 minutes.
11. Transfer the cooked wrap to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
12. If the batter thickens while sitting, blend it for a few seconds before cooking the next wrap.
13. Fill the wraps with your preferred protein, vegetables, sauces, and other toppings.

recipes.title: Seared Scallops with Squash Puree and Sage Butter
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds large sea scallops, tendons removed
2 pounds butternut squash, peeled, seeded, and cut into 1-inch pieces
recipes.body:
## Notes

Dry sea scallops are preferred because they do not contain chemical additives and brown more effectively than wet scallops.

Drying the scallops thoroughly and allowing them to sit at room temperature for 10 minutes helps create a deep golden-brown crust.

Cook the scallops in two batches to avoid overcrowding the skillet.

The squash puree can be refrigerated for up to 3 days.

Extra-large shrimp can be substituted for the scallops.

For easier preparation, peel and cut the butternut squash, mince the shallot and sage, and juice the lemon in advance.

## Instructions

1. Place the scallops on a large plate lined with a triple layer of paper towels.
2. Cover with additional paper towels and gently press to thoroughly dry the scallops.
3. Let the scallops sit at room temperature for 10 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, place the butternut squash in a microwave-safe bowl and cover.
5. Microwave for 8-12 minutes, stirring halfway through, until completely tender.
6. Drain any excess liquid and transfer the squash to a food processor.
7. Add 2 tablespoons butter, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and the cayenne pepper.
8. Blend for about 20 seconds, or until completely smooth.
9. Transfer the squash puree to a bowl, season with additional salt and black pepper as needed, and keep warm.
10. Season the scallops with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt and black pepper.
11. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a 12-inch nonstick skillet over high heat.
12. Add half of the scallops in a single layer, flat side down.
13. Cook without moving for 1 1/2-2 minutes, or until deeply golden brown.
14. Flip the scallops and cook for another 30-90 seconds, or until the sides are firm and the centers are opaque.
15. Transfer the cooked scallops to a plate and loosely cover with aluminum foil.
16. Wipe the skillet clean with paper towels.
17. Melt another 1 tablespoon butter and repeat with the remaining scallops.
18. Transfer the remaining scallops to the plate and keep warm.
19. Reduce the heat to medium and melt the remaining 3 tablespoons butter in the empty skillet.
20. Cook for 1-2 minutes, swirling the skillet constantly, until the butter begins to brown and develops a nutty aroma.
21. Add the minced shallot and sage and cook for about 1 minute, or until fragrant.
22. Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the lemon juice.
23. Divide the warm squash puree among plates and arrange the seared scallops on top or alongside.
24. Spoon the sage brown butter sauce over the scallops and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Scissor-Cut Pasta
recipes.body:
## Notes

Resting the dough for 10 minutes is optional, but it will make the pasta slightly softer and easier to cut.

For one serving, the pasta can be cut directly into the boiling water.

When making multiple servings, cut the pasta pieces onto a floured board first so they can all be added to the boiling water at approximately the same time.

Finish cooking the pasta directly in your preferred sauce.

## Instructions

1. Place the flour, egg, and salt in a bowl or on a clean work surface.
2. Mix until a rough dough forms.
3. Knead the dough until soft and smooth.
4. Optionally, cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes while preparing your pasta sauce.
5. Bring a pot of generously salted water to a boil.
6. Hold the dough over the boiling water and use clean kitchen scissors to snip small pieces directly into the pot.
7. Boil the pasta for 3-4 minutes, or until cooked to your preferred texture.
8. Transfer the pasta directly to your sauce of choice.
9. Toss the pasta with the sauce and cook briefly until well coated.
10. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Vegan Spicy Thai Peanut Ramen
recipes.body:
## Notes

Whisking the peanut butter with a portion of the broth before adding it to the pot helps it blend smoothly into the soup.

Cook the ramen just before serving to prevent the noodles from becoming overly soft.

Customize the soup with your favorite vegetables, tofu, mushrooms, or bok choy.

## Instructions

1. Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
2. Add the chopped garlic and grated ginger and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
3. Stir in the green curry paste and cook for another minute.
4. Pour in 3 cups of the vegetable broth and the coconut milk. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer.
5. In a medium bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 cup of vegetable broth and the peanut butter until smooth.
6. Pour the peanut butter mixture into the pot and whisk until fully incorporated.
7. Add the soy sauce, agave syrup, and lime juice.
8. Simmer gently for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to develop.
9. Taste and adjust the seasoning with additional soy sauce, lime juice, agave, or salt if desired.
10. Add the ramen noodles and cook according to the package directions, usually 1-2 minutes.
11. Divide into bowls and top with cilantro, chopped peanuts, tofu, vegetables, or your preferred toppings.
12. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Spinach and Cannellini Bean Gnocchi
ingredients: 3 frozen cubes spinach or approximately 250 g fresh spinach
1/2 can cannellini beans, with some of their liquid
vegan stracciatella or other soft cheese, for serving
recipes.body:
## Notes

Blend the cannellini beans with some of their liquid to create a smooth and creamy sauce without adding cream.

If using frozen spinach, thaw it before blending. If using fresh spinach, wilt it briefly before blending for a smoother sauce.

Adjust the amount of bean liquid as needed to reach your preferred sauce consistency.

## Instructions

1. Add the spinach, cannellini beans with some of their liquid, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to a blender.
2. Blend until completely smooth and creamy, adding additional bean liquid or water if needed. Set aside.
3. Heat a generous drizzle of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
4. Add the minced garlic and chopped chili and sauté for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
5. Add the precooked gnocchi directly to the pan.
6. Cook the gnocchi, stirring occasionally, until golden brown and lightly crispy on the outside.
7. Pour the spinach and cannellini bean sauce into the pan.
8. Stir until the gnocchi are evenly coated.
9. Simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce is heated through and has thickened slightly.
10. Taste and adjust the seasoning with additional salt, black pepper, or lemon juice as needed.
11. Divide among bowls and top with vegan stracciatella.
12. Finish with lemon zest, chopped chili, and a generous drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Tiramisu Balls
recipes.body:
## Notes

Chill the tiramisu mixture before coating if it is too soft to shape easily.

Keep the tiramisu balls refrigerated until ready to serve.

This recipe contains raw egg. Use a pasteurized egg if serving to pregnant people, young children, older adults, or anyone with a weakened immune system.

## Instructions

1. Separate the egg white from the yolk.
2. Beat the egg white until stiff peaks form.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolk with the vanilla sugar until the mixture becomes pale and creamy.
4. Add the mascarpone to the egg yolk mixture and mix until smooth.
5. Gently fold the beaten egg white into the mascarpone mixture.
6. Add the coffee and gently mix until incorporated.
7. Finely crush or blend the ladyfinger biscuits.
8. Add the crushed ladyfingers to the mascarpone mixture and mix by hand until evenly combined.
9. Shape the mixture into 10 balls, or into balls of your preferred size.
10. Place the tiramisu balls in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to firm up.
11. Add the unsweetened cocoa powder to a small bowl.
12. Roll each chilled tiramisu ball in the cocoa powder until completely coated.
13. Refrigerate until ready to serve.

recipes.title: Greek Yogurt Guacamole
recipes.body:
## Notes

Greek yogurt makes the guacamole creamier and adds a little extra protein.

Adjust the amount of lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to taste.

For a chunkier guacamole, mash the avocado lightly and gently fold in the remaining ingredients.

## Instructions

1. Cut the avocado in half, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a bowl.
2. Mash the avocado with a fork to your preferred consistency.
3. Add the diced tomato and Greek yogurt.
4. Drizzle with lemon juice and season with salt and black pepper.
5. Mix until combined.
6. Taste and adjust the lemon juice, salt, and black pepper as needed.
7. Serve immediately.
